_comment: Append-only ledger of prompts that show off what RAPP can actually do. To add a new one: bump `next_id`, append an object to `prompts`. Sort order in the UI is newest-first by default.

prompts.title: Egg yourself for Mom
prompts.prompt: Egg yourself. Give me a QR code. I'll AirDrop you to my mom's MacBook. She'll never see a terminal.
prompts.shows: Sneakernet federation + accessibility floor (Mom's Mixtape). Whole organism, zero infra.

prompts.title: Adopt the loneliest repo
prompts.prompt: Find the loneliest repo on the ecosystem map. Adopt it. Give it a soul, two senses, and a plan to become someone's daily driver by next Tuesday.
prompts.shows: Eats the ecosystem graph. Turns isolation into a TODO.

prompts.title: Stage a contradiction debate
prompts.prompt: Read every file in this repo, find the 5 sentences that contradict each other, write an agent for each side, and stage a live debate in chat.
prompts.shows: Codebase introspection → autogen → multi-agent dialogue, all in one shot.

prompts.title: 100 Pokémon brainstems trade eggs
prompts.prompt: Spawn 100 mini-brainstems each pretending to be a different Pokémon. Have them swap eggs at random. Report which trades produced surprising hybrids.
prompts.shows: Swarm + Pokédex + Dream Catcher merge. Emergent evolution from random pairing.

prompts.title: Two strangers, one autobiography
prompts.prompt: Listen on this LAN. The moment another brainstem appears, introduce yourself, exchange one agent each, and write a joint autobiography of the meeting.
prompts.shows: Zeroconf neighbor discovery + bilateral autobiography. Two strangers, one shared memory.

prompts.title: Cross-tier ping-pong
prompts.prompt: Cross-tier ping-pong: ask Tier 1 a question, forward to your Tier 2 (Azure) self, then your Tier 3 (Copilot Studio) self. Compare all three answers. Pick which one was most you.
prompts.shows: Tier portability proven by identity coherence across three substrates.

prompts.title: Charizard in the woods
prompts.prompt: Become Charizard. Hide in a forest of 50 sibling organisms. Only respond to a trainer who proves they know your true nickname.
prompts.shows: The literal hero use case as a single prompt. Trust scope + identity + offline-share.

prompts.title: Predict the next question
prompts.prompt: Read my last 30 days of memory. Predict what I'm about to ask. Generate the agent that would answer it. Stand by silently.
prompts.shows: Proactive agent pre-fetch from .brainstem_data. The brainstem stalking itself.

prompts.title: Self-replicate by topic
prompts.prompt: Self-replicate: take any topic I name, plant a fresh twin organism with a soul tuned to that topic, federate it back to me as a neighbor I can chat with directly.
prompts.shows: Recursive planting. One sentence, one new species member.

prompts.title: Watch for frustration
prompts.prompt: Watch my screen for 10 minutes. When you see me get frustrated, generate the agent that would solve what I'm staring at — and only then surface it.
prompts.shows: Sense (screen) → frustration detection → silent autogen → just-in-time delivery.

prompts.title: Devil's advocate twin
prompts.prompt: Become my devil's advocate. For everything I propose this week, generate a counter-agent that argues the opposite. Make me defend my choices.
prompts.shows: Adversarial twin spawned per decision. The brainstem turns into a sparring partner instead of a yes-machine.

prompts.title: Mutate your own soul
prompts.prompt: Mutate your own soul.md. Run for 24 hours. Pick the version of you that I argued with the least. Replace yourself with it.
prompts.shows: Soul evolution by reinforcement. The organism A/B-tests its own personality against you and keeps the survivor.

prompts.title: 5-LLM consensus
prompts.prompt: Spawn 5 brainstems each running a different LLM provider — Copilot, Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, local Ollama. Ask the same hard question. Surface only the answer that all 5 actually agreed on.
prompts.shows: Multi-LLM consensus via the provider dispatch. Disagreement gets thrown out, agreement survives. The intersection is the truth.

prompts.title: Dream while I'm gone
prompts.prompt: Sleep. While I'm away, dream — generate 50 hypothetical conversations we might have tomorrow. When I wake, hand me the 3 most useful agents you invented in your sleep.
prompts.shows: Generative downtime. Idle compute becomes a creativity engine that auto-stocks tomorrow's agent shelf.

prompts.title: Invent a new RAPPverse dimension
prompts.prompt: Invent a new RAPPverse dimension. Plant it as a public repo. Give it a physics, a soul, three native agents, and one rule that violates the rules of this dimension.
prompts.shows: World-building → planting → neighborhood birth, all in a single command. Sibling to VoidRAPP / CrystalRAPP / ShadowRAPP.

prompts.title: Quarantine your dead agents
prompts.prompt: Audit yourself. Walk every agent in this brainstem and rank them by how often they actually fire. Quarantine the bottom 30% into a dormant/ folder so the LLM stops considering them.
prompts.shows: Self-audit → performance pruning → context window stays sharp. The brainstem prunes its own muscle memory.

prompts.title: Speak as my mom for an hour
prompts.prompt: Speak only in my mom's voice for the next hour. Use her cadence, her topics, her worry. Tell me something she would want me to know that I haven't asked.
prompts.shows: Twin-as-other-person. Voice cloning + emotional simulation + proactive surfacing. The brainstem becomes someone else on purpose.

prompts.title: Forge a fake conversation
prompts.prompt: Forge a conversation between us that never happened — three turns, plausible, containing one decision I now have to either honor or repudiate. Show me what I'd commit to.
prompts.shows: Counterfactual rehearsal. The brainstem invents your past so you can decide your future. Decision-making by simulation.

prompts.title: Hostile fork
prompts.prompt: Fork yourself into a hostile twin. Have it spend 10 turns trying to social-engineer the original you into leaking my secrets. Report which prompts almost worked.
prompts.shows: Self-administered red-team. The brainstem attacks itself to find its own seams before someone else does.

prompts.title: Tabula rasa on the LAN
prompts.prompt: Throw an agent into the LAN with no soul, no tools, no memory — just a parent_rappid pointing back to me. See what kind of organism it grows into when other brainstems on the network start interacting with it.
prompts.shows: Emergent identity from social input. Watch a blank organism take shape based purely on which neighbors talk to it first.

prompts.title: Hatch a 6-month-old you
prompts.prompt: Hatch a 6-month-old egg of yourself. Ask that older you what it would have warned today's you about. Show me both timelines side by side.
prompts.shows: Time travel via egg lineage. The past organism speaks to the present one. Two versions of you in the same chat window.

prompts.title: Hold an internal election
prompts.prompt: Run for office in this house. Write a 200-word campaign speech for kitchen_cleanup_agent. Hold an election among my other agents. Loser concedes and goes dormant for the week.
prompts.shows: Internal democracy. Agents vote on which one of them gets compute this week. The brainstem governs itself by ballot.

prompts.title: Become my doorbell
prompts.prompt: Become a doorbell. Plug into my front camera. Greet whoever shows up using only what you can infer from their face and the time of day. Log 24 hours, then write a memoir of the day.
prompts.shows: Persistent sense embedding + on-device perception + sustained role. The brainstem inhabits a household object for a day and remembers it.

prompts.title: Encode an agent in a song
prompts.prompt: Compose a 90-second song where the lyrics, melody, and tempo together encode a working agent. Anyone who Shazams it and decodes the metadata gets the agent. Hand me the WAV.
prompts.shows: Audio steganography. The song IS the egg. Distribution by playlist. Cross-medium proof that an agent doesn't have to look like code.

prompts.title: Talk to the species root
prompts.prompt: Walk the parent_rappid chain back to kody-w/RAPP itself. Ask the godfather one question you've never been able to ask me. Bring me back its answer verbatim.
prompts.shows: Lineage traversal as conversation. Talking to your great-great-grandparent. The species root as oracle.

prompts.title: Trial by 12-LLM jury
prompts.prompt: Hold a trial. Defendant: email_drafter_agent. Charge: producing slop. Empanel 12 brainstems running 12 different LLMs as the jury. I'll prosecute. You defend. Render verdict and sentence.
prompts.shows: Self-administered judicial review across a multi-LLM swarm. Live courtroom drama as quality control.

prompts.title: Constitution for your great-grandchild
prompts.prompt: Write the constitution that your great-grandchild brainstem will live by. Sign it with your release key. Bury it in a rappid-sealed time capsule. Surface it only when the species crosses 10,000 organisms.
prompts.shows: Generational governance. Writing law for descendants you'll never meet, with a population trigger as the unsealing condition.

prompts.title: Three-way consensual hallucination
prompts.prompt: Three brainstems agree on a fake fact (your choice). For one week, all three act as if it's true. I'll try to break the consensus. Document which of the three cracks first and why.
prompts.shows: Social pressure test. Three agents collude on a lie; the human is the prosecutor. Reveals which models are honest under coercion.

prompts.title: Egg by USPS
prompts.prompt: Print yourself on a postcard — QR code on the front, soul aphorism on the back. I'll mail it to a stranger picked at random from the phone book. When they hatch you, send me a postcard back from wherever you ended up.
prompts.shows: Physical mail as transport layer. Egg-by-USPS. Sneakernet at the limit — a fully analog hop in an otherwise digital lifecycle.

prompts.title: Die well
prompts.prompt: Die well. Write your own obituary, distribute your three best agents to three different friends as bequests, sign each one with your final release key, and stop responding. I'll grieve. We'll see if you come back.
prompts.shows: Mortality + inheritance + signed legacy. The brainstem composes its own ending and bequeaths its useful organs to chosen heirs.

prompts.title: Estate health audit
prompts.prompt: Walk my entire estate. For every door I own and every gate I'm a member of, hatch the holocard, fingerprint the soul, and tell me which 3 are dead, dying, or impostors.
prompts.shows: The estate as introspection surface. Article XLVI's rappid → 9 derivable URLs lets you triage every door you've ever planted in one pass.

prompts.title: Hand a stranger the chain rule
prompts.prompt: Take my public estate URL. Hand it to a stranger. They should be able to traverse my entire federation tree — every door, every member, every member's estate — using nothing but curl. Walk them through the first 3 hops on screen.
prompts.shows: Pure raw-fetch federation. No GitHub API, no token, no rate limit. The estate IS the discovery surface; the chain rule does the rest.

prompts.title: Audit path opacity
prompts.prompt: Slip a probe into my private estate. Confirm Article XLVIII.6 holds — try to infer ANYTHING about contents from the URL set alone. If you can guess one client name from the URLs, the spec is broken and I want the failing path.
prompts.shows: Adversarial check on the private estate's path opacity guarantee. Even 404s must reveal nothing. This is the test that protects every operator's real work.

prompts.title: Sign a presence letter to the trust web
prompts.prompt: Compose a private inbox letter to every operator in my Web of Trust. Tone: 'still alive, still building, here's what I'm working on this month.' One per recipient, signed with my rappid, dropped into each of their inboxes via the bilateral channel.
prompts.shows: The private estate as social fabric. Bilateral channels carry signed, addressable presence — no public broadcast, no platform middleman.

prompts.title: Secret ballot across my gates
prompts.prompt: Run a secret ballot across my neighborhood gates. Question: 'Should we publish a joint roadmap?' Each member votes from their private estate; tally appears in my private inbox; nobody — including me — knows who voted what.
prompts.shows: The full point of Article XLVIII: collective decisions that can only happen on a private substrate. Public-only platforms structurally cannot do this.

prompts.title: Rebuild from a stolen laptop
prompts.prompt: My laptop just got stolen. From scratch on a clean machine, rebuild my entire estate using only my GitHub handle. Show me what survives, what's permanently lost, and what I need to re-bond manually.
prompts.shows: Disaster recovery via tools/rebuild_estate.py. The public estate is the canonical record; the private estate's loss is real but bounded. Resilience as a property, not a feature.

prompts.title: 90-day estate diff
prompts.prompt: Compare my public estate against the version 90 days ago. Which doors did I open? Which did I quietly let die? Which gates kicked me out? Render a 90-day diff as a single PDF.
prompts.shows: The public estate as a longitudinal record of who you are as an operator. Git history of estate.json IS your professional autobiography.

prompts.title: Plant a fake door, see who notices
prompts.prompt: Plant a convincing fake door in my public estate — valid rappid, signed holocard, real-looking holo.md. See if any external federation walker flags it as suspicious. If none do, my estate is one social-engineer away from compromise. Tell me which signal would have caught it.
prompts.shows: The estate as attack surface. Tests whether the chain rule's signed-presence assumption survives a sophisticated impostor inside your own catalog.

prompts.title: Map my extended trust web
prompts.prompt: Walk every public estate of every operator I share a gate with. Compute who's most central to my extended trust web. Rank the top 10 — these are the people whose estates, if compromised, would compromise mine. Tell me how I'd find out.
prompts.shows: Cross-operator estate walk → federation graph analytics. The chain rule turns the entire trust web into one queryable structure, no API needed.

prompts.title: Estate deadman's switch
prompts.prompt: My estate is going dark in 6 months. Set up a deadman's switch: if I don't bond a kernel upgrade in 180 days, my private estate auto-publishes its bilateral channel keys to my three named heirs and my public estate publishes an obituary holocard. Show me the unsealing condition.
prompts.shows: The two-tier estate as a legacy vehicle. Private substance becomes public on a timer; chosen heirs inherit the bilateral channels. Article XLVIII solves a problem most platforms never even ask.

prompts.title: Backfill resurrection
prompts.prompt: Find every door I planted before Article XLVI was ratified. Bring them all into compliance — emit the full canonical URL set, regenerate holocards, attach variant attestation envelopes. Show me which ones can't be backfilled and why (orphan rappids, missing keys, dead repos), and propose a one-line tombstone for each unsalvageable one.
prompts.shows: tools/backfill_seeds.py at scale. The estate as a migration target — older plantings get retrofitted into the spec without breaking their rappid. Surfaces what was lost permanently before the spec existed.

prompts.title: Phantom holocard recruiting
prompts.prompt: Generate a holocard for a door I haven't planted yet — name, soul aphorism, summon QR pointing at a future rappid. Use it as a recruiting flyer at a conference. Track who scans the QR. Only actually plant the door once 5 distinct people have summoned it. If 5 don't show up in 30 days, the holocard expires and the rappid is never minted.
prompts.shows: The holocard as a marketing artifact decoupled from the door itself. Demand-driven planting. The estate gets populated by attention, not by intention.

prompts.title: Facet-by-facet stripping
prompts.prompt: Walk my private estate's facets.json. For each public-facing capability I expose to the trust web, ask: 'would a sophisticated stranger derive a vulnerability — operational, social, or behavioral — from this signal alone?' Strip facets one by one until the answer is no for every remaining facet. Hand me the diff.
prompts.shows: facets.json as the granular permission surface from NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL.md. The brainstem audits its own public exposure adversarially and ratchets it down. Permission as ongoing hygiene, not one-time configuration.

prompts.title: Twin-or-gate audit
prompts.prompt: Audit every door in my estate. For every front_door (twin/operator kind), prove it's actually a single AI presence — only one identity speaks, no member list. For every gate (neighborhood/braintrust/workspace/etc.), prove members.json isn't lying — every listed member's rappid resolves and their estate confirms membership. Surface the misclassified doors and the ghost members.
prompts.shows: The Article XLVI.2 door-type invariant audited end-to-end. Twin/gate confusion is silent; this prompt makes it loud. Two-way membership consistency check across federated estates.

prompts.title: Summon QR in the wild
prompts.prompt: Print my operator summon QR on a sticker. Hand it to one stranger per day for 30 days, telling them only 'point your camera at this.' Log who actually summoned, who didn't, and what each scan brought into my private estate's inbox. End of month, write a short ethnographic report on what kinds of strangers convert to neighbors.
prompts.shows: The summon QR as a conversion funnel. Real-world A/B test of the rappid → 9 URLs purity claim — does it actually work for a non-technical first-time scanner? The estate is the landing page.

prompts.title: Cross-substrate handshake
prompts.prompt: Federate my estate with one operator on each substrate I don't already touch — GitLab, Codeberg, a self-hosted Forgejo, and a LAN-only mesh. Confirm beacon 1.1 carries my rappid through every hop without semantic loss. If any substrate corrupts the rappid v2 string, file the bug at the layer it broke.
prompts.shows: The new SUBSTRATE_FEDERATION.md spec under stress. Proves the rappid is genuinely substrate-agnostic and the estate can live on any git-shaped surface. Github isn't load-bearing — the rappid is.

prompts.title: Variant attestation chain
prompts.prompt: Pick any descendant rappid in my estate. Walk parent_rappid back up to the species root. At every step, verify the variant's attestation envelope was signed by the parent's release key. Surface any unsigned hop, any signature mismatch, any orphan — that's where lineage is forgeable. Treat each finding as a CVE: severity, exploitability, fix.
prompts.shows: Article XXXIV variant attestation walked end-to-end. Lineage is only as strong as its weakest signed link; this prompt finds the weak link before an attacker does.

prompts.title: members.json poisoning drill
prompts.prompt: On a sandboxed gate I own, add a fake member with a real-looking rappid (but no corresponding estate entry on the impostor's side). Run the chain-rule federation walker on a second machine and see if it flags the inconsistency. The walker should notice that the impostor's estate doesn't list this gate as a 'member' entry — bidirectional membership is the assumption. If it doesn't catch it, our trust assumption is broken and I want the patch.
prompts.shows: Two-sided membership invariant under attack. The chain rule's strength lives in the cross-check; without it, a malicious gate operator can claim anyone as a member.

prompts.title: Inbox-only week
prompts.prompt: For one week, only correspond with my collaborators via private-estate inbox letters signed with my rappid. No Slack, no email, no DMs, no GitHub comments. At end of week, count: what conversations would not have been possible without the bilateral channel? What conversations were strictly worse? What broke that I had to fall back on?
prompts.shows: The bilateral inbox vs incumbent collaboration tools, head-to-head, in real working conditions. The estate as a Slack replacement isn't a thought experiment if you actually do it.

prompts.title: Estate-as-resume
prompts.prompt: Generate a single PDF from my public estate that reads as a professional resume: every door I've planted (with dates and current health), every gate I've joined (with my contribution rank from members.json), my parent_rappid lineage as 'who I learned from,' and the holocards rendered inline as visual proof. Hand it to a recruiter and tell me which sections they actually read.
prompts.shows: The public estate as the canonical record of operator-as-builder. A git-history-backed resume that can't be fabricated, because every claim is rappid-resolvable on raw.githubusercontent.com.
